title: NOC 72202 – Power system electricians | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 72202 professionals are the high-voltage technical specialists who maintain the backbone of Canada's electrical grid. As a TEER 2 occupation, these electricians install, maintain, test, and repair the complex equipment found in power generation stations, transmission substations, and distribution networks. In 2026, as Canada executes its "Clean Electricity Regulations" to achieve a net-zero grid, Power System Electricians are classified as "Critical Utility Talent." For immigration, this role is a premier candidate for Express Entry Category-Based Selection (Trades) and the Saskatchewan SINP energy streams.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level2Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group722 – Electrical trades and electrical power line and cable workersMinor Group7220 – Electricians (except industrial and power system)NOC Code72202

duties: Equipment Installation: Install and maintain heavy electrical distribution equipment such as transformers, generators, voltage regulators, switches, and circuit breakers.
SCADA Management: Install and maintain Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) systems used to monitor grid performance remotely.
Powerhouse Maintenance: Service and repair electrical apparatus within generating stations and powerhouses to ensure continuous supply.
Testing & Fault Finding: Use voltmeters, ammeters, and other specialized high-voltage test instruments to locate electrical faults and verify system operation.
Relay Protection: Calibrate and test protective relay systems that shield the grid from surges and faults.
Reporting: Complete detailed test and maintenance reports to ensure compliance with federal and provincial safety standards.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is mandatory.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a four-year apprenticeship program specifically for power system electricians is the standard route.
Work Experience: Alternatively, over four years of work experience in the trade combined with college or industry courses in electrical technology may be accepted.
Trade Certification (Voluntary): * Trade certification is available but voluntary in Alberta, Manitoba, Yukon, Northwest Territories, and Nunavut.
Red Seal Endorsement: While not mandatory in all regions, obtaining the Red Seal is the gold standard for PR applications and allows you to work across all Canadian provinces.
Physical stamina for climbing and handling heavy materials, as well as high-level troubleshooting skills, are essential.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, annual salaries for experienced Power System Electricians in major cities like Calgary or Toronto often range from $96,000 to $125,000 inclusive of overtime.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate that the utilities sector will face a significant wave of retirements over the next decade. As Canada integrates millions of new Electric Vehicles (EVs) into the grid, the demand for electricians capable of managing the resulting increase in transmission and distribution loads ensures long-term job security through 2033.
labour_market_demand: The demand for Power System Electricians is Good to Very Good across Canada in 2026. Ontario, Quebec, and Alberta are reporting steady growth due to massive investments in grid modernization and renewable energy integration. According to 2026 Job Bank data, approximately 85% of these professionals work directly in the Utilities sector. With a small pool of unemployed workers and a high volume of specialized infrastructure projects (such as new hydroelectric and nuclear refurbishments), qualified international applicants have a significant competitive advantage.
The "Clean Grid" Revolution: Lead the transition to a sustainable future by managing the infrastructure for wind, solar, and next-generation nuclear power.
Top-Tier Compensation: This is widely considered the highest-paying electrical specialization in Canada, reflecting the high-voltage risks and specialized technical knowledge required.
Exceptional Stability: Over 64% of workers in this NOC are employed year-round in essential utility roles, offering unparalleled job security.
Direct PR Pathway: Power System Electricians are a core part of the Express Entry "Trade" category draws, often receiving invitations to apply for PR with lower CRS scores.
pathways: Express Entry (Category-Based Selection): Highly targeted under the Trade Occupations category in 2026.
New 2026 TR to PR Pathway: A temporary measure targeting 33,000 skilled workers already in Canada, specifically focusing on essential resource and utility sectors.
Saskatchewan SINP (Occupations In-Demand): A high-priority route for power electricians supporting the province's extensive utility network.
Alberta Opportunity Stream: Ideal for those currently working in the province's energy and power generation sectors.
